option.name: updatedContent
updatedContent
option.value:
# Payment System - Low Level Design (LLD)
## 0) One-Page Visual Flow (Single Master Box Diagram)
```text +------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | DIGITAL WALLET + REWARDS SYSTEM (SINGLE BOX VIEW) | | | | USER / CLIENT (Web, Mobile, Postman) | | | | | v | | +----------------------------------------------- API GATEWAY :8062 ------------------------------------------------+ | | | Entry Point + Route Mapping + JWT Validate + RBAC + Rate Limit + Swagger Aggregation | | | | Public: /api/auth/**, /swagger-ui/**, /v3/api-docs/** | | | | Protected: all business APIs | | | | Admin-only: /api/admin/**, /api/campaigns/**, /api/kyc/pending, /api/kyc/{userId}/status | | | +---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | | | | | | | | | | | | | | v v v v | | +------------------------+ +------------------------+ +------------------------+ +------------------------+ | | | AUTH SERVICE :8063 | | USER-KYC SERVICE :8064 | | WALLET SERVICE :8065 | | REWARDS SERVICE :8066 | | | | - signup | | - create user | | - balance | | - summary | | | | - login -> JWT | | - submit KYC(docId) | | - topup / transfer | | - catalog | | | | - OTP generate/verify | | - upload KYC file | | - txn history | | - redeem | | | | | | (pdf/doc/docx/jpg/png)| | | | | | | +-----------+------------+ | - pending list (ADMIN) | +-----------+------------+ +-----------+------------+ | | | | - approve/reject status | | | | | | +------------+-------------+ | | | | | | | | | | v v v v | | +------------------+ +------------------+ +------------------+ +------------------+ | | | wallet_auth_db | | wallet_user_db | | wallet_db | | rewards_db | | | +------------------+ +------------------+ +------------------+ +------------------+ | | | | +-------------------------------------+ +-------------------------------------+ | | | ADMIN SERVICE :8067 | | TRANSACTION SERVICE :8068 | | | | - pending KYC view | | - topup / transfer / payment/refund| | | | - approve/reject KYC orchestration | | - create txn: PENDING | | | | - campaigns + dashboard | | - process -> SUCCESS / FAILED | | | +-------------------+----------------+ | - ledger entries (DEBIT/CREDIT) | | | | +-------------------+----------------+ | | v | | | +------------------+ v | | | admin_db | +------------------+ | | +------------------+ | transaction_db | | | +---------+--------+ | | | | | v | | +------------------+ | | | ledger_entries | | | +------------------+ | | | | ASYNC EVENT BUS: | | +--------------------------------------------- RabbitMQ :5672 / 15672 --------------------------------------------------+ | | | Wallet <--> RabbitMQ <--> Rewards ; Transaction --publish--> RabbitMQ --consume--> Rewards/Wallet side effects | | | +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | | | | PLATFORM BACKBONE: | | +----------------------+ +----------------------+ | | | CONFIG SERVER :8060 | ---------> | All services config | | | +----------------------+ +----------------------+ | | +----------------------+ +----------------------+ | | | EUREKA SERVER :8061 | <--------> | Service Discovery | | | +----------------------+ +----------------------+ | | | | SECURITY DECISION INSIDE GATEWAY (same request path): | | Request -> Public? yes=forward ; no=JWT check ; invalid=401 ; admin-route+non-admin=403 ; over limit=429 ; else forward to target service | +------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ ```
---
## Task Receipt + Plan - [x] Capture detailed LLD for current implementation (as-is code). - [x] Add architecture-level + component-level figures for clarity. - [x] Document API contracts, internal layers, data model, and security internals. - [x] Map inter-service sync/async flows and runtime deployment details.
---
## 1) Scope and Traceability This LLD complements `HIGH_LEVEL_DESIGN.md` and explains internal design for: - `api-gateway` - `auth-service` - `user-kyc-service` - `wallet-service` - `rewards-service` - `admin-service` - `transaction-service` - `config-server`, `eureka-server`
Focus is on current code and config behavior in this repo.
---
## 2) Architecture Layers (Common Pattern) Each domain service follows mostly this layering: 1. `controller` - REST endpoints, validation trigger, request mapping. 2. `service` - business rules, orchestration, state transitions. 3. `repository` - JPA data access. 4. `entity` - persistence model. 5. `dto` - API payload contracts. 6. `config/security` - OpenAPI, JWT helpers, filter/interceptor wiring.
```mermaid flowchart LR Client --> Gateway Gateway --> Controller Controller --> Service Service --> Repository Repository --> PostgreSQL Service --> MQ[RabbitMQ (where used)] ```
---
## 3) Component Architecture (Detailed)
### 3.1 API Gateway (`8062`) Key responsibilities: - Route-based forwarding from `github-config/api-gateway.yml`. - JWT auth + role checks in `JwtAuthGlobalFilter`. - In-memory throttling in `SimpleRateLimitFilter`. - Swagger aggregation `/swagger-ui.html` + `/v3/api-docs/<service>`.
**Core internals** - `JwtAuthGlobalFilter` - Skips public paths (`/api/auth`, `/swagger-ui`, `/v3/api-docs`, etc.). - Validates Bearer token. - Extracts `role`, `userId`, `subject`. - Enforces admin-only routes: - `/api/admin/**` - `/api/campaigns/**` - `/api/kyc/pending` - `/api/kyc/{userId}/status` - Adds propagated headers: - `X-Authenticated-User` - `X-Authenticated-Role` - `X-Authenticated-UserId` - `SimpleRateLimitFilter` - Sliding 60s window counter per user/IP. - Default `gateway.rate-limit.requests-per-minute=120`. - Returns `429` + `Retry-After: 60` on breach.
```mermaid flowchart TD RQ[Request] --> P{Public Path?} P -- yes --> RT[Route] P -- no --> J{JWT valid?} J -- no --> E401[401] J -- yes --> A{Admin route?} A -- yes --> AR{role==ADMIN?} AR -- no --> E403[403] AR -- yes --> RL[Rate Limit] A -- no --> RL RL --> LIM{within limit?} LIM -- no --> E429[429] LIM -- yes --> RT ```
### 3.2 Auth Service (`8063`) Controllers: - `AuthController` - `POST /api/auth/signup` - `POST /api/auth/login` - plus test endpoints (`/test`, `/test/user`) - `OtpController` - `POST /api/auth/otp/generate` - `POST /api/auth/otp/verify` - `GET /api/auth/otp/health`
Design notes: - Signup returns token as `null` and asks user to login for token. - Login returns JWT. - Password hashing via bcrypt flow in auth service logic. - OTP flow supports email/SMS path in controller/service.
### 3.3 User-KYC Service (`8064`) Controller: `UserKycController` - `POST /api/users` - `GET /api/users/{id}` - `POST /api/kyc/submit/{userId}` (form field: `documentId`) - `POST /api/kyc/upload/{userId}` (multipart field: `document`) - `GET /api/kyc/pending` (ADMIN) - `PUT /api/kyc/{userId}/status` (ADMIN)
Security model: - Gateway enforces admin routes. - Service-level defense via `ensureAdmin(...)`: - checks `X-Authenticated-Role` - fallback: parses JWT via `JwtRoleValidator`
Entity `UserProfile` now contains: - profile fields: `id`, `fullName`, `email`, `phone` - kyc fields: `kycStatus`, `kycDocumentId` - upload metadata: `kycDocumentName`, `kycDocumentContentType`, `kycDocumentSize` - blob: `kycDocumentData` (`@Lob`, hidden from API via `@JsonIgnore`)
Validation rules: - upload required, allowed MIME types: - `application/pdf` - `application/msword` - `application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document` - `image/jpeg`, `image/png` - multipart limits configured to `10MB`.
### 3.4 Wallet Service (`8065`) Controller: `WalletController` - `GET /api/wallet/balance?userId=` - `POST /api/wallet/topup` - `POST /api/wallet/transfer` - `GET /api/wallet/transactions?userId=`
Design intent: - Exposes balance and ledger/history operations. - Integrates with event/messaging layer where applicable.
### 3.5 Rewards Service (`8066`) Controller: `RewardsController` - `GET /api/rewards/summary?userId=` - `GET /api/rewards/catalog` - `POST /api/rewards/redeem`
Design intent: - Maintains points account and redemption catalog behavior. - Consumes transaction/wallet-related reward triggers in async flow.
### 3.6 Admin Service (`8067`) Controller: `AdminController` - `GET /api/admin/kyc/pending` - `POST /api/admin/kyc/{userId}/approve` - `POST /api/admin/campaigns` - `GET /api/admin/dashboard`
Internal interaction: - Uses Feign client to call user-kyc endpoints. - `FeignAuthForwardingConfig` forwards auth headers downstream.
### 3.7 Transaction Service (`8068`) Controller: `TransactionController` - `POST /transactions/topup` - `POST /transactions/transfer` - `POST /transactions/payment` - `POST /transactions/refund` - `GET /transactions/{id}` - `GET /transactions/user/{userId}` - `GET /transactions/history?from=&to=`
Design intent: - Handles transaction lifecycle (`PENDING` -> `SUCCESS/FAILED`). - Persists ledger entries for accounting/audit. - Publishes side-effect events to MQ for rewards/wallet integration where configured.
---
## 0.1 RabbitMQ Usage (Single Box Diagram)
```text +----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | RABBITMQ FLOW IN THIS PROJECT (BOX VIEW) | +----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | | | Synchronous API entry (via Gateway) | | +---------------------+ +----------------------+ +---------------------------+ | | | Client / Admin App | ---> | API Gateway :8062 | ---> | Business Services | | | +---------------------+ +----------------------+ | (Wallet/Rewards/Txn) | | | +-------------+-------------+ | | | | | | publish / consume events | | v | | +----------------------------------------------------- RabbitMQ :5672 (UI :15672) -------------------------------------------+ | | | | | | | Producer Side | | | | - Transaction Service :8068 | | | | - On SUCCESS flow, publishes domain events after txn + ledger persistence | | | | - Wallet Service :8065 (where wallet async event hooks are used) | | | | | | | | Broker Internals (logical flow) | | | | [Exchange] ---> [Queue(s)] ---> [Consumer services] | | | | | | | | Consumer Side | | | | - Rewards Service :8066 | | | | - Consumes events to update points behavior | | | | - Wallet Service :8065 | | | | - Consumes relevant events for wallet-side async updates | | | +------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | | | | | v | | Persistent data impact | | +------------------+ +------------------+ +------------------+ | | | transaction_db | | rewards_db | | wallet_db | | | | (txn + ledger) | | (points updates) | | (wallet updates) | | | +------------------+ +------------------+ +------------------+ | | | | Why RabbitMQ here: decouple side-effects, reduce tight service coupling, improve resilience for cross-domain async workflows. | +----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ ```
---
## Task Receipt + Plan - [x] Capture detailed LLD for current implementation (as-is code). - [x] Add architecture-level + component-level figures for clarity. - [x] Document API contracts, internal layers, data model, and security internals. - [x] Map inter-service sync/async flows and runtime deployment details.
---
## 1) Scope and Traceability This LLD complements `HIGH_LEVEL_DESIGN.md` and explains internal design for: - `api-gateway` - `auth-service` - `user-kyc-service` - `wallet-service` - `rewards-service` - `admin-service` - `transaction-service` - `config-server`, `eureka-server`
Focus is on current code and config behavior in this repo.
---
## 2) Architecture Layers (Common Pattern) Each domain service follows mostly this layering: 1. `controller` - REST endpoints, validation trigger, request mapping. 2. `service` - business rules, orchestration, state transitions. 3. `repository` - JPA data access. 4. `entity` - persistence model. 5. `dto` - API payload contracts. 6. `config/security` - OpenAPI, JWT helpers, filter/interceptor wiring.
```mermaid flowchart LR Client --> Gateway Gateway --> Controller Controller --> Service Service --> Repository Repository --> PostgreSQL Service --> MQ[RabbitMQ (where used)] ```
---
## 3) Component Architecture (Detailed)
### 3.1 API Gateway (`8062`) Key responsibilities: - Route-based forwarding from `github-config/api-gateway.yml`. - JWT auth + role checks in `JwtAuthGlobalFilter`. - In-memory throttling in `SimpleRateLimitFilter`. - Swagger aggregation `/swagger-ui.html` + `/v3/api-docs/<service>`.
**Core internals** - `JwtAuthGlobalFilter` - Skips public paths (`/api/auth`, `/swagger-ui`, `/v3/api-docs`, etc.). - Validates Bearer token. - Extracts `role`, `userId`, `subject`. - Enforces admin-only routes: - `/api/admin/**` - `/api/campaigns/**` - `/api/kyc/pending` - `/api/kyc/{userId}/status` - Adds propagated headers: - `X-Authenticated-User` - `X-Authenticated-Role` - `X-Authenticated-UserId` - `SimpleRateLimitFilter` - Sliding 60s window counter per user/IP. - Default `gateway.rate-limit.requests-per-minute=120`. - Returns `429` + `Retry-After: 60` on breach.
```mermaid flowchart TD RQ[Request] --> P{Public Path?} P -- yes --> RT[Route] P -- no --> J{JWT valid?} J -- no --> E401[401] J -- yes --> A{Admin route?} A -- yes --> AR{role==ADMIN?} AR -- no --> E403[403] AR -- yes --> RL[Rate Limit] A -- no --> RL RL --> LIM{within limit?} LIM -- no --> E429[429] LIM -- yes --> RT ```
### 3.2 Auth Service (`8063`) Controllers: - `AuthController` - `POST /api/auth/signup` - `POST /api/auth/login` - plus test endpoints (`/test`, `/test/user`) - `OtpController` - `POST /api/auth/otp/generate` - `POST /api/auth/otp/verify` - `GET /api/auth/otp/health`
Design notes: - Signup returns token as `null` and asks user to login for token. - Login returns JWT. - Password hashing via bcrypt flow in auth service logic. - OTP flow supports email/SMS path in controller/service.
### 3.3 User-KYC Service (`8064`) Controller: `UserKycController` - `POST /api/users` - `GET /api/users/{id}` - `POST /api/kyc/submit/{userId}` (form field: `documentId`) - `POST /api/kyc/upload/{userId}` (multipart field: `document`) - `GET /api/kyc/pending` (ADMIN) - `PUT /api/kyc/{userId}/status` (ADMIN)
Security model: - Gateway enforces admin routes. - Service-level defense via `ensureAdmin(...)`: - checks `X-Authenticated-Role` - fallback: parses JWT via `JwtRoleValidator`
Entity `UserProfile` now contains: - profile fields: `id`, `fullName`, `email`, `phone` - kyc fields: `kycStatus`, `kycDocumentId` - upload metadata: `kycDocumentName`, `kycDocumentContentType`, `kycDocumentSize` - blob: `kycDocumentData` (`@Lob`, hidden from API via `@JsonIgnore`)
Validation rules: - upload required, allowed MIME types: - `application/pdf` - `application/msword` - `application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document` - `image/jpeg`, `image/png` - multipart limits configured to `10MB`.
### 3.4 Wallet Service (`8065`) Controller: `WalletController` - `GET /api/wallet/balance?userId=` - `POST /api/wallet/topup` - `POST /api/wallet/transfer` - `GET /api/wallet/transactions?userId=`
Design intent: - Exposes balance and ledger/history operations. - Integrates with event/messaging layer where applicable.
### 3.5 Rewards Service (`8066`) Controller: `RewardsController` - `GET /api/rewards/summary?userId=` - `GET /api/rewards/catalog` - `POST /api/rewards/redeem`
Design intent: - Maintains points account and redemption catalog behavior. - Consumes transaction/wallet-related reward triggers in async flow.
### 3.6 Admin Service (`8067`) Controller: `AdminController` - `GET /api/admin/kyc/pending` - `POST /api/admin/kyc/{userId}/approve` - `POST /api/admin/campaigns` - `GET /api/admin/dashboard`
Internal interaction: - Uses Feign client to call user-kyc endpoints. - `FeignAuthForwardingConfig` forwards auth headers downstream.
### 3.7 Transaction Service (`8068`) Controller: `TransactionController` - `POST /transactions/topup` - `POST /transactions/transfer` - `POST /transactions/payment` - `POST /transactions/refund` - `GET /transactions/{id}` - `GET /transactions/user/{userId}` - `GET /transactions/history?from=&to=`
Design intent: - Handles transaction lifecycle (`PENDING` -> `SUCCESS/FAILED`). - Persists ledger entries for accounting/audit. - Publishes side-effect events to MQ for rewards/wallet integration where configured.
---
## 0.1 RabbitMQ Usage (Single Box Diagram)
```text +----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | RABBITMQ FLOW IN THIS PROJECT (BOX VIEW) | +----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | | | Synchronous API entry (via Gateway) | | +---------------------+ +----------------------+ +---------------------------+ | | | Client / Admin App | ---> | API Gateway :8062 | ---> | Business Services | | | +---------------------+ +----------------------+ | (Wallet/Rewards/Txn) | | | +-------------+-------------+ | | | | | | publish / consume events | | v | | +----------------------------------------------------- RabbitMQ :5672 (UI :15672) -------------------------------------------+ | | | | | | | Producer Side | | | | - Transaction Service :8068 | | | | - On SUCCESS flow, publishes domain events after txn + ledger persistence | | | | - Wallet Service :8065 (where wallet async event hooks are used) | | | | | | | | Broker Internals (logical flow) | | | | [Exchange] ---> [Queue(s)] ---> [Consumer services] | | | | | | | | Consumer Side | | | | - Rewards Service :8066 | | | | - Consumes events to update points behavior | | | | - Wallet Service :8065 | | | | - Consumes relevant events for wallet-side async updates | | | +------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | | | | | v | | Persistent data impact | | +------------------+ +------------------+ +------------------+ | | | transaction_db | | rewards_db | | wallet_db | | | | (txn + ledger) | | (points updates) | | (wallet updates) | | | +------------------+ +------------------+ +------------------+ | | | | Why RabbitMQ here: decouple side-effects, reduce tight service coupling, improve resilience for cross-domain async workflows. | +----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ ```
---
## Task Receipt + Plan - [x] Capture detailed LLD for current implementation (as-is code). - [x] Add architecture-level + component-level figures for clarity. - [x] Document API contracts, internal layers, data model, and security internals. - [x] Map inter-service sync/async flows and runtime deployment details.
---
## 1) Scope and Traceability This LLD complements `HIGH_LEVEL_DESIGN.md` and explains internal design for: - `api-gateway` - `auth-service` - `user-kyc-service` - `wallet-service` - `rewards-service` - `admin-service` - `transaction-service` - `config-server`, `eureka-server`
Focus is on current code and config behavior in this repo.
---
## 2) Architecture Layers (Common Pattern) Each domain service follows mostly this layering: 1. `controller` - REST endpoints, validation trigger, request mapping. 2. `service` - business rules, orchestration, state transitions. 3. `repository` - JPA data access. 4. `entity` - persistence model. 5. `dto` - API payload contracts. 6. `config/security` - OpenAPI, JWT helpers, filter/interceptor wiring.
```mermaid flowchart LR Client --> Gateway Gateway --> Controller Controller --> Service Service --> Repository Repository --> PostgreSQL Service --> MQ[RabbitMQ (where used)] ```
---
## 3) Component Architecture (Detailed)
### 3.1 API Gateway (`8062`) Key responsibilities: - Route-based forwarding from `github-config/api-gateway.yml`. - JWT auth + role checks in `JwtAuthGlobalFilter`. - In-memory throttling in `SimpleRateLimitFilter`. - Swagger aggregation `/swagger-ui.html` + `/v3/api-docs/<service>`.
**Core internals** - `JwtAuthGlobalFilter` - Skips public paths (`/api/auth`, `/swagger-ui`, `/v3/api-docs`, etc.). - Validates Bearer token. - Extracts `role`, `userId`, `subject`. - Enforces admin-only routes: - `/api/admin/**` - `/api/campaigns/**` - `/api/kyc/pending` - `/api/kyc/{userId}/status` - Adds propagated headers: - `X-Authenticated-User` - `X-Authenticated-Role` - `X-Authenticated-UserId` - `SimpleRateLimitFilter` - Sliding 60s window counter per user/IP. - Default `gateway.rate-limit.requests-per-minute=120`. - Returns `429` + `Retry-After: 60` on breach.
```mermaid flowchart TD RQ[Request] --> P{Public Path?} P -- yes --> RT[Route] P -- no --> J{JWT valid?} J -- no --> E401[401] J -- yes --> A{Admin route?} A -- yes --> AR{role==ADMIN?} AR -- no --> E403[403] AR -- yes --> RL[Rate Limit] A -- no --> RL RL --> LIM{within limit?} LIM -- no --> E429[429] LIM -- yes --> RT ```
### 3.2 Auth Service (`8063`) Controllers: - `AuthController` - `POST /api/auth/signup` - `POST /api/auth/login` - plus test endpoints (`/test`, `/test/user`) - `OtpController` - `POST /api/auth/otp/generate` - `POST /api/auth/otp/verify` - `GET /api/auth/otp/health`
Design notes: - Signup returns token as `null` and asks user to login for token. - Login returns JWT. - Password hashing via bcrypt flow in auth service logic. - OTP flow supports email/SMS path in controller/service.
### 3.3 User-KYC Service (`8064`) Controller: `UserKycController` - `POST /api/users` - `GET /api/users/{id}` - `POST /api/kyc/submit/{userId}` (form field: `documentId`) - `POST /api/kyc/upload/{userId}` (multipart field: `document`) - `GET /api/kyc/pending` (ADMIN) - `PUT /api/kyc/{userId}/status` (ADMIN)
Security model: - Gateway enforces admin routes. - Service-level defense via `ensureAdmin(...)`: - checks `X-Authenticated-Role` - fallback: parses JWT via `JwtRoleValidator`
Entity `UserProfile` now contains: - profile fields: `id`, `fullName`, `email`, `phone` - kyc fields: `kycStatus`, `kycDocumentId` - upload metadata: `kycDocumentName`, `kycDocumentContentType`, `kycDocumentSize` - blob: `kycDocumentData` (`@Lob`, hidden from API via `@JsonIgnore`)
Validation rules: - upload required, allowed MIME types: - `application/pdf` - `application/msword` - `application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document` - `image/jpeg`, `image/png` - multipart limits configured to `10MB`.
### 3.4 Wallet Service (`8065`) Controller: `WalletController` - `GET /api/wallet/balance?userId=` - `POST /api/wallet/topup` - `POST /api/wallet/transfer` - `GET /api/wallet/transactions?userId=`
Design intent: - Exposes balance and ledger/history operations. - Integrates with event/messaging layer where applicable.
### 3.5 Rewards Service (`8066`) Controller: `RewardsController` - `GET /api/rewards/summary?userId=` - `GET /api/rewards/catalog` - `POST /api/rewards/redeem`
Design intent: - Maintains points account and redemption catalog behavior. - Consumes transaction/wallet-related reward triggers in async flow.
### 3.6 Admin Service (`8067`) Controller: `AdminController` - `GET /api/admin/kyc/pending` - `POST /api/admin/kyc/{userId}/approve` - `POST /api/admin/campaigns` - `GET /api/admin/dashboard`
Internal interaction: - Uses Feign client to call user-kyc endpoints. - `FeignAuthForwardingConfig` forwards auth headers downstream.
### 3.7 Transaction Service (`8068`) Controller: `TransactionController` - `POST /transactions/topup` - `POST /transactions/transfer` - `POST /transactions/payment` - `POST /transactions/refund` - `GET /transactions/{id}` - `GET /transactions/user/{userId}` - `GET /transactions/history?from=&to=`
Design intent: - Handles transaction lifecycle (`PENDING` -> `SUCCESS/FAILED`). - Persists ledger entries for accounting/audit. - Publishes side-effect events to MQ for rewards/wallet integration where configured.
---
## 0.1 RabbitMQ Usage (Single Box Diagram)
```text +----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | RABBITMQ FLOW IN THIS PROJECT (BOX VIEW) | +----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | | | Synchronous API entry (via Gateway) | | +---------------------+ +----------------------+ +---------------------------+ | | | Client / Admin App | ---> | API Gateway :8062 | ---> | Business Services | | | +---------------------+ +----------------------+ | (Wallet/Rewards/Txn) | | | +-------------+-------------+ | | | | | | publish / consume events | | v | | +----------------------------------------------------- RabbitMQ :5672 (UI :15672) -------------------------------------------+ | | | | | | | Producer Side | | | | - Transaction Service :8068 | | | | - On SUCCESS flow, publishes domain events after txn + ledger persistence | | | | - Wallet Service :8065 (where wallet async event hooks are used) | | | | | | | | Broker Internals (logical flow) | | | | [Exchange] ---> [Queue(s)] ---> [Consumer services] | | | | | | | | Consumer Side | | | | - Rewards Service :8066 | | | | - Consumes events to update points behavior | | | | - Wallet Service :8065 | | | | - Consumes relevant events for wallet-side async updates | | | +------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | | | | | v | | Persistent data impact | | +------------------+ +------------------+ +------------------+ | | | transaction_db | | rewards_db | | wallet_db | | | | (txn + ledger) | | (points updates) | | (wallet updates) | | | +------------------+ +------------------+ +------------------+ | | | | Why RabbitMQ here: decouple side-effects, reduce tight service coupling, improve resilience for cross-domain async workflows. | +----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ ```
---
## Task Receipt + Plan - [x] Capture detailed LLD for current implementation (as-is code). - [x] Add architecture-level + component-level figures for clarity. - [x] Document API contracts, internal layers, data model, and security internals. - [x] Map inter-service sync/async flows and runtime deployment details.
---
## 1) Scope and Traceability This LLD complements `HIGH_LEVEL_DESIGN.md` and explains internal design for: - `api-gateway` - `auth-service` - `user-kyc-service` - `wallet-service` - `rewards-service` - `admin-service` - `transaction-service` - `config-server`, `eureka-server`
Focus is on current code and config behavior in this repo.
---
## 2) Architecture Layers (Common Pattern) Each domain service follows mostly this layering: 1. `controller` - REST endpoints, validation trigger, request mapping. 2. `service` - business rules, orchestration, state transitions. 3. `repository` - JPA data access. 4. `entity` - persistence model. 5. `dto` - API payload contracts. 6. `config/security` - OpenAPI, JWT helpers, filter/interceptor wiring.
```mermaid flowchart LR Client --> Gateway Gateway --> Controller Controller --> Service Service --> Repository Repository --> PostgreSQL Service --> MQ[RabbitMQ (where used)] ```
---
## 3) Component Architecture (Detailed)
### 3.1 API Gateway (`8062`) Key responsibilities: - Route-based forwarding from `github-config/api-gateway.yml`. - JWT auth + role checks in `JwtAuthGlobalFilter`. - In-memory throttling in `SimpleRateLimitFilter`. - Swagger aggregation `/swagger-ui.html` + `/v3/api-docs/<service>`.
**Core internals** - `JwtAuthGlobalFilter` - Skips public paths (`/api/auth`, `/swagger-ui`, `/v3/api-docs`, etc.). - Validates Bearer token. - Extracts `role`, `userId`, `subject`. - Enforces admin-only routes: - `/api/admin/**` - `/api/campaigns/**` - `/api/kyc/pending` - `/api/kyc/{userId}/status` - Adds propagated headers: - `X-Authenticated-User` - `X-Authenticated-Role` - `X-Authenticated-UserId` - `SimpleRateLimitFilter` - Sliding 60s window counter per user/IP. - Default `gateway.rate-limit.requests-per-minute=120`. - Returns `429` + `Retry-After: 60` on breach.
```mermaid flowchart TD RQ[Request] --> P{Public Path?} P -- yes --> RT[Route] P -- no --> J{JWT valid?} J -- no --> E401[401] J -- yes --> A{Admin route?} A -- yes --> AR{role==ADMIN?} AR -- no --> E403[403] AR -- yes --> RL[Rate Limit] A -- no --> RL RL --> LIM{within limit?} LIM -- no --> E429[429] LIM -- yes --> RT ```
### 3.2 Auth Service (`8063`) Controllers: - `AuthController` - `POST /api/auth/signup` - `POST /api/auth/login` - plus test endpoints (`/test`, `/test/user`) - `OtpController` - `POST /api/auth/otp/generate` - `POST /api/auth/otp/verify` - `GET /api/auth/otp/health`
Design notes: - Signup returns token as `null` and asks user to login for token. - Login returns JWT. - Password hashing via bcrypt flow in auth service logic. - OTP flow supports email/SMS path in controller/service.
### 3.3 User-KYC Service (`8064`) Controller: `UserKycController` - `POST /api/users` - `GET /api/users/{id}` - `POST /api/kyc/submit/{userId}` (form field: `documentId`) - `POST /api/kyc/upload/{userId}` (multipart field: `document`) - `GET /api/kyc/pending` (ADMIN) - `PUT /api/kyc/{userId}/status` (ADMIN)
Security model: - Gateway enforces admin routes. - Service-level defense via `ensureAdmin(...)`: - checks `X-Authenticated-Role` - fallback: parses JWT via `JwtRoleValidator`
Entity `UserProfile` now contains: - profile fields: `id`, `fullName`, `email`, `phone` - kyc fields: `kycStatus`, `kycDocumentId` - upload metadata: `kycDocumentName`, `kycDocumentContentType`, `kycDocumentSize` - blob: `kycDocumentData` (`@Lob`, hidden from API via `@JsonIgnore`)
Validation rules: - upload required, allowed MIME types: - `application/pdf` - `application/msword` - `application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document` - `image/jpeg`, `image/png` - multipart limits configured to `10MB`.
### 3.4 Wallet Service (`8065`) Controller: `WalletController` - `GET /api/wallet/balance?userId=` - `POST /api/wallet/topup` - `POST /api/wallet/transfer` - `GET /api/wallet/transactions?userId=`
Design intent: - Exposes balance and ledger/history operations. - Integrates with event/messaging layer where applicable.
### 3.5 Rewards Service (`8066`) Controller: `RewardsController` - `GET /api/rewards/summary?userId=` - `GET /api/rewards/catalog` - `POST /api/rewards/redeem`
Design intent: - Maintains points account and redemption catalog behavior. - Consumes transaction/wallet-related reward triggers in async flow.
### 3.6 Admin Service (`8067`) Controller: `AdminController` - `GET /api/admin/kyc/pending` - `POST /api/admin/kyc/{userId}/approve` - `POST /api/admin/campaigns` - `GET /api/admin/dashboard`
Internal interaction: - Uses Feign client to call user-kyc endpoints. - `FeignAuthForwardingConfig` forwards auth headers downstream.
### 3.7 Transaction Service (`8068`) Controller: `TransactionController` - `POST /transactions/topup` - `POST /transactions/transfer` - `POST /transactions/payment` - `POST /transactions/refund` - `GET /transactions/{id}` - `GET /transactions/user/{userId}` - `GET /transactions/history?from=&to=`
Design intent: - Handles transaction lifecycle (`PENDING` -> `SUCCESS/FAILED`). - Persists ledger entries for accounting/audit. - Publishes side-effect events to MQ for rewards/wallet integration where configured.
---
## 0.1 RabbitMQ Usage (Single Box Diagram)
```text +----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | RABBITMQ FLOW IN THIS PROJECT (BOX VIEW) | +----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | | | Synchronous API entry (via Gateway) | | +---------------------+ +----------------------+ +---------------------------+ | | | Client / Admin App | ---> | API Gateway :8062 | ---> | Business Services | | | +---------------------+ +----------------------+ | (Wallet/Rewards/Txn) | | | +-------------+-------------+ | | | | | | publish / consume events | | v | | +----------------------------------------------------- RabbitMQ :5672 (UI :15672) -------------------------------------------+ | | | | | | | Producer Side | | | | - Transaction Service :8068 | | | | - On SUCCESS flow, publishes domain events after txn + ledger persistence | | | | - Wallet Service :8065 (where wallet async event hooks are used) | | | | | | | | Broker Internals (logical flow) | | | | [Exchange] ---> [Queue(s)] ---> [Consumer services] | | | | | | | | Consumer Side | | | | - Rewards Service :8066 | | | | - Consumes events to update points behavior | | | | - Wallet Service :8065 | | | | - Consumes relevant events for wallet-side async updates | | | +------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | | | | | v | | Persistent data impact | | +------------------+ +------------------+ +------------------+ | | | transaction_db | | rewards_db | | wallet_db | | | | (txn + ledger) | | (points updates) | | (wallet updates) | | | +------------------+ +------------------+ +------------------+ | | | | Why RabbitMQ here: decouple side-effects, reduce tight service coupling, improve resilience for cross-domain async workflows. | +----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ ```
---
## Task Receipt + Plan - [x] Capture detailed LLD for current implementation (as-is code). - [x] Add architecture-level + component-level figures for clarity. - [x] Document API contracts, internal layers, data model, and security internals. - [x] Map inter-service sync/async flows and runtime deployment details.
---
## 1) Scope and Traceability This LLD complements `HIGH_LEVEL_DESIGN.md` and explains internal design for: - `api-gateway` - `auth-service` - `user-kyc-service` - `wallet-service` - `rewards-service` - `admin-service` - `transaction-service` - `config-server`, `eureka-server`
Focus is on current code and config behavior in this repo.
---
## 2) Architecture Layers (Common Pattern) Each domain service follows mostly this layering: 1. `controller` - REST endpoints, validation trigger, request mapping. 2. `service` - business rules, orchestration, state transitions. 3. `repository` - JPA data access. 4. `entity` - persistence model. 5. `dto` - API payload contracts. 6. `config/security` - OpenAPI, JWT helpers, filter/interceptor wiring.
```mermaid flowchart LR Client --> Gateway Gateway --> Controller Controller --> Service Service --> Repository Repository --> PostgreSQL Service --> MQ[RabbitMQ (where used)] ```
---
## 3) Component Architecture (Detailed)
### 3.1 API Gateway (`8062`) Key responsibilities: - Route-based forwarding from `github-config/api-gateway.yml`. - JWT auth + role checks in `JwtAuthGlobalFilter`. - In-memory throttling in `SimpleRateLimitFilter`. - Swagger aggregation `/swagger-ui.html` + `/v3/api-docs/<service>`.
**Core internals** - `JwtAuthGlobalFilter` - Skips public paths (`/api/auth`, `/swagger-ui`, `/v3/api-docs`, etc.). - Validates Bearer token. - Extracts `role`, `userId`, `subject`. - Enforces admin-only routes: - `/api/admin/**` - `/api/campaigns/**` - `/api/kyc/pending` - `/api/kyc/{userId}/status` - Adds propagated headers: - `X-Authenticated-User` - `X-Authenticated-Role` - `X-Authenticated-UserId` - `SimpleRateLimitFilter` - Sliding 60s window counter per user/IP. - Default `gateway.rate-limit.requests-per-minute=120`. - Returns `429` + `Retry-After: 60` on breach.
```mermaid flowchart TD RQ[Request] --> P{Public Path?} P -- yes --> RT[Route] P -- no --> J{JWT valid?} J -- no --> E401[401] J -- yes --> A{Admin route?} A -- yes --> AR{role==ADMIN?} AR -- no --> E403[403] AR -- yes --> RL[Rate Limit] A -- no --> RL RL --> LIM{within limit?} LIM -- no --> E429[429] LIM -- yes --> RT ```
### 3.2 Auth Service (`8063`) Controllers: - `AuthController` - `POST /api/auth/signup` - `POST /api/auth/login` - plus test endpoints (`/test`, `/test/user`) - `OtpController` - `POST /api/auth/otp/generate` - `POST /api/auth/otp/verify` - `GET /api/auth/otp/health`
Design notes: - Signup returns token as `null` and asks user to login for token. - Login returns JWT. - Password hashing via bcrypt flow in auth service logic. - OTP flow supports email/SMS path in controller/service.
### 3.3 User-KYC Service (`8064`) Controller: `UserKycController` - `POST /api/users` - `GET /api/users/{id}` - `POST /api/kyc/submit/{userId}` (form field: `documentId`) - `POST /api/kyc/upload/{userId}` (multipart field: `document`) - `GET /api/kyc/pending` (ADMIN) - `PUT /api/kyc/{userId}/status` (ADMIN)
Security model: - Gateway enforces admin routes. - Service-level defense via `ensureAdmin(...)`: - checks `X-Authenticated-Role` - fallback: parses JWT via `JwtRoleValidator`
Entity `UserProfile` now contains: - profile fields: `id`, `fullName`, `email`, `phone` - kyc fields: `kycStatus`, `kycDocumentId` - upload metadata: `kycDocumentName`, `kycDocumentContentType`, `kycDocumentSize` - blob: `kycDocumentData` (`@Lob`, hidden from API via `@JsonIgnore`)
Validation rules: - upload required, allowed MIME types: - `application/pdf` - `application/msword` - `application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document` - `image/jpeg`, `image/png` - multipart limits configured to `10MB`.
### 3.4 Wallet Service (`8065`) Controller: `WalletController` - `GET /api/wallet/balance?userId=` - `POST /api/wallet/topup` - `POST /api/wallet/transfer` - `GET /api/wallet/transactions?userId=`
Design intent: - Exposes balance and ledger/history operations. - Integrates with event/messaging layer where applicable.
### 3.5 Rewards Service (`8066`) Controller: `RewardsController` - `GET /api/rewards/summary?userId=` - `GET /api/rewards/catalog` - `POST /api/rewards/redeem`
Design intent: - Maintains points account and redemption catalog behavior. - Consumes transaction/wallet-related reward triggers in async flow.
### 3.6 Admin Service (`8067`) Controller: `AdminController` - `GET /api/admin/kyc/pending` - `POST /api/admin/kyc/{userId}/approve` - `POST /api/admin/campaigns` - `GET /api/admin/dashboard`
Internal interaction: - Uses Feign client to call user-kyc endpoints. - `FeignAuthForwardingConfig` forwards auth headers downstream.
### 3.7 Transaction Service (`8068`) Controller: `TransactionController` - `POST /transactions/topup` - `POST /transactions/transfer` - `POST /transactions/payment` - `POST /transactions/refund` - `GET /transactions/{id}` - `GET /transactions/user/{userId}` - `GET /transactions/history?from=&to=`
Design intent: - Handles transaction lifecycle (`PENDING` -> `SUCCESS/FAILED`). - Persists ledger entries for accounting/audit. - Publishes side-effect events to MQ for rewards/wallet integration where configured.
---
## 0.2 Service-Wise Separate Box Diagrams
### A) Admin Service (`8067`) - Full Box View ```text +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | ADMIN SERVICE :8067 | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | API Layer | | - GET /api/admin/kyc/pending | | - POST /api/admin/kyc/{userId}/approve | | - POST /api/admin/campaigns | | - GET /api/admin/dashboard | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | Core Components | | - AdminController | | - AdminService | | - UserKycClient (Feign -> user-kyc-service) | | - FeignAuthForwardingConfig (forwards Authorization + role headers) | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | Domain / Data | | - Campaign entity + related repository | | - admin_db | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | Security and Access | | - Gateway-level ADMIN route protection | | - Works with downstream KYC admin-only update flow | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | External Dependencies | | - API Gateway (incoming) | | - user-kyc-service (pending/list + status update orchestration) | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ ```
### B) API Gateway (`8062`) - Box View ```text +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | API GATEWAY :8062 | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | Responsibilities | | - Single entry point | | - Route mapping to all business services | | - JWT validation + RBAC | | - Rate limiting | | - Aggregated Swagger docs | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | Key Components | | - JwtAuthGlobalFilter | | - JwtTokenValidator | | - SimpleRateLimitFilter | | - SwaggerRedirectController | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | Config/Routes | | - /api/auth/** -> auth-service | | - /api/users/**,/api/kyc/** -> user-kyc-service | | - /api/wallet/** -> wallet-service | | - /api/rewards/** -> rewards-service | | - /api/admin/**,/api/campaigns/** -> admin-service | | - /transactions/** -> transaction-service | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ ```
### C) Auth Service (`8063`) - Box View ```text +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | AUTH SERVICE :8063 | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | API Layer | | - POST /api/auth/signup | | - POST /api/auth/login | | - POST /api/auth/otp/generate | | - POST /api/auth/otp/verify | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | Core Components | | - AuthController | | - OtpController | | - AuthService | | - OtpService | | - JwtService | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | Data + Security | | - AuthUser / repository | | - password hashing (bcrypt) | | - JWT issue at login | | - wallet_auth_db | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ ```
### D) User-KYC Service (`8064`) - Box View ```text +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | USER-KYC SERVICE :8064 | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | API Layer | | - POST /api/users | | - GET /api/users/{id} | | - POST /api/kyc/submit/{userId} (documentId) | | - POST /api/kyc/upload/{userId} (multipart document) | | - GET /api/kyc/pending (ADMIN) | | - PUT /api/kyc/{userId}/status (ADMIN) | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | Core Components | | - UserKycController | | - UserKycService | | - JwtRoleValidator (fallback admin check) | | - RestExceptionHandler | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | Data Model | | - UserProfile | | - KYC status fields + upload metadata + blob | | - wallet_user_db | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ ```
### E) Wallet Service (`8065`) - Box View ```text +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | WALLET SERVICE :8065 | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | API Layer | | - GET /api/wallet/balance?userId= | | - POST /api/wallet/topup | | - POST /api/wallet/transfer | | - GET /api/wallet/transactions?userId= | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | Core Components | | - WalletController | | - WalletService | | - wallet repositories/entities | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | Integration | | - RabbitMQ producer/consumer hooks (wallet async flows) | | - wallet_db | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ ```
### F) Rewards Service (`8066`) - Box View ```text +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | REWARDS SERVICE :8066 | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | API Layer | | - GET /api/rewards/summary?userId= | | - GET /api/rewards/catalog | | - POST /api/rewards/redeem | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | Core Components | | - RewardsController | | - RewardsService | | - rewards repositories/entities | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | Integration | | - RabbitMQ consumer for reward events | | - rewards_db | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ ```
### G) Transaction Service (`8068`) - Box View ```text +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | TRANSACTION SERVICE :8068 | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | API Layer | | - POST /transactions/topup | | - POST /transactions/transfer | | - POST /transactions/payment | | - POST /transactions/refund | | - GET /transactions/{id} | | - GET /transactions/user/{userId} | | - GET /transactions/history?from=&to= | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | Core Components | | - TransactionController | | - TransactionService | | - transaction + ledger repositories/entities | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | Transaction Logic | | - Lifecycle: PENDING -> SUCCESS / FAILED | | - Ledger entries for accounting | | - RabbitMQ publish for async side-effects | | - transaction_db | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ ```
### H) Infrastructure Boxes (Support) ```text +--------------------------------------+ +--------------------------------------+ +--------------------------------------+ | CONFIG SERVER :8060 | | EUREKA SERVER :8061 | | RABBITMQ :5672 / UI :15672 | | - Centralized config | | - Service discovery registry | | - Exchange / Queue / Consumer model | +--------------------------------------+ +--------------------------------------+ +--------------------------------------+
+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | POSTGRES :5432 (Logical DB split) | | wallet_auth_db | wallet_user_db | wallet_db | rewards_db | admin_db | transaction_db | +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ ```
---
## 0.3 Inter-Service Communication Diagram (Who talks to whom, and through what)
```text +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | INTER-SERVICE COMMUNICATION MAP (SYNC + ASYNC) | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | | | [Client Apps] | | | | | | HTTP | | v | | [API Gateway :8062] (Single Entry + Routing + JWT/RBAC + Rate Limit) | | | | | |--HTTP--> [Auth Service :8063] (Signup/Login/OTP) | | |--HTTP--> [User-KYC Service :8064] (Users/KYC submit/upload/pending/status) | | |--HTTP--> [Wallet Service :8065] (Balance/Topup/Transfer/History) | | |--HTTP--> [Rewards Service :8066] (Summary/Catalog/Redeem) | | |--HTTP--> [Admin Service :8067] (Admin KYC/Campaigns/Dashboard) | | |--HTTP--> [Transaction Service :8068] (Topup/Transfer/Payment/Refund/History) | | | | Admin orchestration path (synchronous service-to-service): | | [Admin Service :8067] --Feign/HTTP (+ forwarded auth headers)--> [User-KYC Service :8064] | | | | Async event path (message broker): | | [Transaction Service :8068] --publish--> [RabbitMQ] --consume--> [Rewards Service :8066] | | [Wallet Service :8065] <------- publish/consume hooks -------> [RabbitMQ] | | | | Infrastructure communication: | | [Config Server :8060] --config--> [Gateway + Eureka + Services] | | [Eureka Server :8061] <---service registration/discovery---> [Gateway + Services] | | | | Data communication (service-owned databases): | | [Auth Service] <--> [wallet_auth_db] | | [User-KYC Service] <--> [wallet_user_db] | | [Wallet Service] <--> [wallet_db] | | [Rewards Service] <--> [rewards_db] | | [Admin Service] <--> [admin_db] | | [Transaction Service] <--> [transaction_db + ledger_entries] | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ ```
### Communication Matrix (Quick Reference)
| From | To | Through | Why | |---|---|---|---| | Client | All business services | `API Gateway` (HTTP) | Single entry, security, routing | | API Gateway | Auth/User-KYC/Wallet/Rewards/Admin/Transaction | Route forwarding (HTTP) | Request dispatch | | Admin Service | User-KYC Service | Feign (HTTP) + auth header forwarding | KYC pending/approval orchestration | | Transaction Service | RabbitMQ | Publish event | Async side-effects after transaction flow | | RabbitMQ | Rewards Service | Consumer | Points/reward updates | | RabbitMQ | Wallet Service | Consumer/producer hooks | Wallet async workflows | | Config Server | Gateway + services | Config fetch | Centralized runtime config | | Eureka Server | Gateway + services | Registration/discovery | Service lookup | | Each service | Its own DB | JPA/SQL | Persistent data ownership |
---
## 10) Design Decisions and Trade-offs 1. **Gateway-centric security** simplifies policy control but requires strict route governance. 2. **In-memory rate limit** is simple and fast but not horizontally consistent (future Redis recommended). 3. **KYC file in DB blob** is easy to manage now; object storage is better for scale. 4. **Per-service DB isolation** improves modularity and bounded context ownership. 5. **Async MQ integration** reduces tight coupling for reward and side-effect workflows.
---
## 11) Suggested Next LLD Enhancements 1. Add per-service class diagram blocks (Controller -> Service -> Repository -> Entity). 2. Add API request/response JSON examples section for each endpoint. 3. Add state-machine diagram for KYC and transaction lifecycles. 4. Add observability LLD (logging format, trace IDs, metrics dashboards).

option.value: spring: datasource: url: jdbc:postgresql://postgres:5432/admin_db username: postgres password: root

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
spring: datasource: url: jdbc:postgresql://postgres:5432/admin_db username: postgres password: root jpa: hibernate: ddl-auto: update properties: hibernate: dialect: org.hibernate.dialect.PostgreSQLDialect
eureka: client: service-url: defaultZone: http://eureka-server:8061/eureka/
server: port: 8067
management: endpoints: web: exposure: include: health,info
springdoc: api-docs: enabled: true path: /v3/api-docs swagger-ui: path: /swagger-ui.html

option.name: originalContent
option.value:
server: port: 8067
spring: datasource: url: jdbc:postgresql://localhost:5432/admin_db username: postgres password: root jpa: hibernate: ddl-auto: update properties: hibernate: dialect: org.hibernate.dialect.PostgreSQLDialect

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
server: port: 8067
spring: datasource: url: jdbc:postgresql://localhost:5432/admin_db username: postgres password: root jpa: hibernate: ddl-auto: update properties: hibernate: dialect: org.hibernate.dialect.PostgreSQLDialect
eureka: client: service-url: defaultZone: http://localhost:8061/eureka/
management: endpoints: web: exposure: include: health,info
springdoc: api-docs: enabled: true path: /v3/api-docs swagger-ui: path: /swagger-ui.html

option.name: updatedContent
updatedContent
updatedContent
option.value:
server: port: 8061
eureka: client: register-with-eureka: false fetch-registry: false server: # Disable eviction protection when renewals are below threshold enable-self-preservation: false # Time for which a lease will not be cancelled if heartbeats are lost eviction-interval-timer-in-ms: 5000 # Expected client heartbeat rate expected-client-renewal-interval-seconds: 30 # If the number of heartbeats from the clients becomes less than this threshold renewal-percent-threshold: 0.5
management: endpoints: web: exposure: include: health,info

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
server: port: 8061
eureka: client: register-with-eureka: false fetch-registry: false server: # Disable eviction protection when renewals are below threshold enable-self-preservation: false # Time for which a lease will not be cancelled if heartbeats are lost eviction-interval-timer-in-ms: 5000 # Expected client heartbeat rate expected-client-renewal-interval-seconds: 30 # If the number of heartbeats from the clients becomes less than this threshold renewal-percent-threshold: 0.5
management: endpoints: web: exposure: include: health,info

option.name: updatedContent
